trans-unit.source: Exercise 5: Aggregate and Assess G2P Money Transfers in Pakistan
trans-unit.target: Exercise 5: Aggregate and Assess G2P Money Transfers in Pakistan

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ The aim of this exercise is to introduce a workflow that combines both spatial and non-spatial processing. A common step is to aggregate data on administrative scales such as adm2 or adm3. This exercise will teach you how to aggregate information about cash transfers on administrative levels and then join the statistics with a polygon data for the administrative boundaries.
trans-unit.target: :::{card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ The aim of this exercise is to introduce a workflow that combines both spatial and non-spatial processing. A common step is to aggregate data on administrative scales such as adm2 or adm3. This exercise will teach you how to aggregate information about cash transfers on administrative levels and then join the statistics with a polygon data for the administrative boundaries.

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: Aggregating and analysing data
trans-unit.target: Aggregating and analysing data

trans-unit.source: Creating situational reports
trans-unit.target: Creating situational reports

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card}

trans-unit.source: __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^ ~ 60 minutes
trans-unit.target: __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^ ~ 60 minutes

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Statistics by Categories](/content/Module_5/en_qgis_non_spatial_tools.md)
trans-unit.target: [Statistics by Categories](/content/Module_5/en_qgis_non_spatial_tools.md)

trans-unit.source: [Non-Spatial Join](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_joins_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Non-Spatial Join](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_joins_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Step-by-step instructions
trans-unit.target: Step-by-step instructions

trans-unit.source: __Click [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_5/Module_5_Exercise_5_aggregating_adm.zip) to download the datasets for this exercise.__
trans-unit.target: __Click [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_5/Exercise_5/Module_5_Exercise_5_aggregating_adm.zip) to download the datasets for this exercise.__

trans-unit.source: `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned.csv` - Data table with cash transactions. This dataset has been cleaned and all personal information has been removed.
trans-unit.target: `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned.csv` - Data table with cash transactions. This dataset has been cleaned and all personal information has been removed.

trans-unit.source: `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909.shp` - Administrative boundaries on adm2 level (districts). :::
trans-unit.target: `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909.shp` - Administrative boundaries on adm2 level (districts). :::

trans-unit.source: Preparing the data
trans-unit.target: Preparing the data

trans-unit.source: Unzip the exercise data and create a new QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Unzip the exercise data and create a new QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Load the data into your QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Load the data into your QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Let's familiarise ourselves with the data. Open the attribute table of each layer and see what kind of information is stored in the datasets.
trans-unit.target: Let's familiarise ourselves with the data. Open the attribute table of each layer and see what kind of information is stored in the datasets.

trans-unit.source: ::::{note} We want to aggregate the information about money transactions on adm3- or adm2-level. Can you identify which column in the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer corresponds to adm2 and adm3?
trans-unit.target: ::::{note} We want to aggregate the information about money transactions on adm3- or adm2-level. Can you identify which column in the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer corresponds to adm2 and adm3?

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} Solution
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} Solution

trans-unit.source: By comparing the values in the column `admin2_EN` from the `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer, we can see that the column `var_attr_03` corresponds to the admin2-level in the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer. By sorting the layers alphabetically, it is easier to find matching values in both attribute tables.
trans-unit.target: By comparing the values in the column `admin2_EN` from the `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer, we can see that the column `var_attr_03` corresponds to the admin2-level in the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer. By sorting the layers alphabetically, it is easier to find matching values in both attribute tables.

trans-unit.source: Step 1: Aggregating the amount of transferred money on admin2
trans-unit.target: Step 1: Aggregating the amount of transferred money on admin2

trans-unit.source: In the processing toolbox, search for the tool `Aggregate` under `Vector Geometry`. <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on it. A new window will open (see {numref}`aggregate_tool`).
trans-unit.target: In the processing toolbox, search for the tool `Aggregate` under `Vector Geometry`. <kbd>Double-Click</kbd> on it. A new window will open (see {numref}`aggregate_tool`).

trans-unit.source: The Aggregate-tool in QGIS 3.36 :::
trans-unit.target: The Aggregate-tool in QGIS 3.36 :::

trans-unit.source: In the "Aggregate"-window,
trans-unit.target: In the "Aggregate"-window,

trans-unit.source: Select the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer as input layer.
trans-unit.target: Select the `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`-layer as input layer.

trans-unit.source: `Group by Expression` is where we select which column we want to have grouped (or selected as category). We want to identify the amount of money transferred to each district (admin2-level), so we need to select the corresponding column. In our case, this is the column `var_attr_03`.
trans-unit.target: `Group by Expression` is where we select which column we want to have grouped (or selected as category). We want to identify the amount of money transferred to each district (admin2-level), so we need to select the corresponding column. In our case, this is the column `var_attr_03`.

trans-unit.source: This box is where we select how the tool aggregates the different columns:
trans-unit.target: This box is where we select how the tool aggregates the different columns:

trans-unit.source: We want to calculate the `Sum` for the column "Amount".
trans-unit.target: We want to calculate the `Sum` for the column "Amount".

trans-unit.source: For the column "var attr 03" we want to select `Concatenate_unique`. This returns all unique strings from a field.
trans-unit.target: For the column "var attr 03" we want to select `Concatenate_unique`. This returns all unique strings from a field.

trans-unit.source: The other columns can be set to `concatenate_unique`
trans-unit.target: The other columns can be set to `concatenate_unique`

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`. A new layer called "Aggregated" will appear in the layers-panel. Close the "Aggregate"-window.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`. A new layer called "Aggregated" will appear in the layers-panel. Close the "Aggregate"-window.

trans-unit.source: Adjust the aggregation function for the relevant columns. Pay attention that the `Type` for the amount is set to "Integer". If you have imported the table correctly into QGIS, this should be set automatically. :::
trans-unit.target: Adjust the aggregation function for the relevant columns. Pay attention that the `Type` for the amount is set to "Integer". If you have imported the table correctly into QGIS, this should be set automatically. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} :type: note If the `Type` for the amount column is set to "Text (string)", this means that QGIS reads the data format for this column as being a string value. QGIS can't perform mathematic operations on string values because they are being read as non-numeric data. Make sure to import the layer through the `Add delimited text layer`-dialogue (`Layer` > `Add Layer` > `Add delimited text layer...`) and make sure the `Type` for the column "Amount" is set to Integer. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} :type: note If the `Type` for the amount column is set to "Text (string)", this means that QGIS reads the data format for this column as being a string value. QGIS can't perform mathematic operations on string values because they are being read as non-numeric data. Make sure to import the layer through the `Add delimited text layer`-dialogue (`Layer` > `Add Layer` > `Add delimited text layer...`) and make sure the `Type` for the column "Amount" is set to Integer. :::

trans-unit.source: Let's take a look at the new layer by opening the attribute table. If you have done everything correctly, the table should look like {numref}`aggregate_results`. We can see on row for each distinct value in the `var attr 03` column (Gwardar, Jamshoro, Dadu, Kambar Shahdadkot, Shiparpur). In the column `Amount` we see the sum of all the individual transfers. In the other columns, we can see a string with the different values of the original table separated by commas (e.g. the different admin3-units, Thesils, under the column `var attr 04`).
trans-unit.target: Let's take a look at the new layer by opening the attribute table. If you have done everything correctly, the table should look like {numref}`aggregate_results`. We can see on row for each distinct value in the `var attr 03` column (Gwardar, Jamshoro, Dadu, Kambar Shahdadkot, Shiparpur). In the column `Amount` we see the sum of all the individual transfers. In the other columns, we can see a string with the different values of the original table separated by commas (e.g. the different admin3-units, Thesils, under the column `var attr 04`).

trans-unit.source: The aggregated data from `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`. :::
trans-unit.target: The aggregated data from `G2P_disbursement_report_cleaned`. :::

trans-unit.source: Step 2: Joining the aggregated data with administrative boundaries
trans-unit.target: Step 2: Joining the aggregated data with administrative boundaries

trans-unit.source: In this step, we want to add the aggregated information we gained from the CSV file to the administrative boundaries. We need to join the aggregated table with the `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer.
trans-unit.target: In this step, we want to add the aggregated information we gained from the CSV file to the administrative boundaries. We need to join the aggregated table with the `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer.

trans-unit.source: In the processing toolbox, search for `Join attributes by field value`. <kbd>Double click</kbd> on it. A new window will open.
trans-unit.target: In the processing toolbox, search for `Join attributes by field value`. <kbd>Double click</kbd> on it. A new window will open.

trans-unit.source: The Join attributes by field value dialogue box in QGIS 3.36. :::
trans-unit.target: The Join attributes by field value dialogue box in QGIS 3.36. :::

trans-unit.source: The input layer should be `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer. This will be the layer that will receive additional information. The geometries of the input layer will be preserved. The `Table field` should be set to "ADM2_EN". These are english names for the admin2-level.
trans-unit.target: The input layer should be `pak_admbnda_adm2_wfp_20220909`-layer. This will be the layer that will receive additional information. The geometries of the input layer will be preserved. The `Table field` should be set to "ADM2_EN". These are english names for the admin2-level.

trans-unit.source: The second input layer should be the `Aggregated` layer from the previous step. `Table field 2` should also be the english names for the administrative boundaries. In our case, the corresponding column is "var attr 03". Under `Layer 2 fields to copy`, only select `amount` as we are not interested in the other values.
trans-unit.target: The second input layer should be the `Aggregated` layer from the previous step. `Table field 2` should also be the english names for the administrative boundaries. In our case, the corresponding column is "var attr 03". Under `Layer 2 fields to copy`, only select `amount` as we are not interested in the other values.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`. A new layer `Joined Layer` will appear in the layer-panel.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`. A new layer `Joined Layer` will appear in the layer-panel.

trans-unit.source: Close the joining dialogue window and investigate the new layer by opening it's attribute table. Scroll the right to find the new column "amount" that has been joined.
trans-unit.target: Close the joining dialogue window and investigate the new layer by opening it's attribute table. Scroll the right to find the new column "amount" that has been joined.

trans-unit.source: Notice how most of the rows have `NULL` as their value in this column? This is because the aggregated table only has 5 distinct districts (adm2) that received money. You can sort the table by clicking on the column header.
trans-unit.target: Notice how most of the rows have `NULL` as their value in this column? This is because the aggregated table only has 5 distinct districts (adm2) that received money. You can sort the table by clicking on the column header.

trans-unit.source: Congratulations, we have successfully joined a CSV-file with a polygon layer!
trans-unit.target: Congratulations, we have successfully joined a CSV-file with a polygon layer!

trans-unit.source: The aggregated amount joined to a layer of the administrative boundaries. :::
trans-unit.target: The aggregated amount joined to a layer of the administrative boundaries. :::
